Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project  Description:    Malibu  Municipal  Code  Section  17.

48.070  requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent to any outdoor pedestrian seating area.  The two locations within the City’s right-of-way and City-owned property that previously were thought to require  a  vehicle  impact  protection  devices.

a8ee2b06-157f-4efe-bc9d-31224b7ece7b.label: text_chunk
a8ee2b06-157f-4efe-bc9d-31224b7ece7b.text:
The  project  has  been  re- evaluated and determined that these locations do not require vehicle impact protection devices per MCC 17.48.070.  The project will be eliminated.

Malibu Bluffs Park South Walkway

Malibu Bluffs Park South Walkway ¾ Project Description:

  This project will include replacing the existing sidewalk located on the south side of Malibu Bluffs Park.

Trancas Canyon Park Playground Resurfacing

¾ Project Description:

  The project will include removing and replacing the upper layer of the playground surfacing at Trancas Canyon Park.

PCH Median Improvements at Paradise Cove and Zuma Beach

¾ Project Description:

  This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes installing new raised medians and improvements.  New raised medians are proposed east and west of PCH and Paradise Cove Road.

1c196f42-228f-44fb-ad24-6d9cb95a8e80.label: text_chunk
1c196f42-228f-44fb-ad24-6d9cb95a8e80.text:
The proposed improvements also include the relocation of the existing bus stop and new signage.

  The project will also include the installation of new raised medians on PCH in the areas where the double yellow lines exist in the vicinity of Zuma Beach, specifically where the yellow paddles are installed.

3fb0f1fe-a696-4c82-a789-498fa1f2eb18.label: text_chunk
3fb0f1fe-a696-4c82-a789-498fa1f2eb18.text:
PCH Crosswalk Improvements at Big Rock Drive and 20326 PCH

¾ Project Description:

 This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes the installation of overhead warning signs and raised medians
Page 4 of 8
Agenda Item # 4.C.on PCH. New flashing warning beacons will be installed approximately 200 feet east and west of 20326 PCH (Moonshadow’s Restaurant).

104a3a3d-bf23-4cc8-a197-34c1e1b401ac.label: text_chunk
104a3a3d-bf23-4cc8-a197-34c1e1b401ac.text:

 A new overhead flashing “signal ahead” beacon will be installed 400 feet east of PCH and Big Rock Drive intersection.

Kanan Dume Biofilter

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  was  identified  in  the  City’s  Enhanced Watershed Management Plan (EWMP).

e090ea95-b979-4701-a2dc-0047d9eb323e.label: text_chunk
e090ea95-b979-4701-a2dc-0047d9eb323e.text:
This project includes the installation of  a  biofilter  on  Kanan  Dume  Drive  to  capture  and  treat  stormwater.  The project  is  intended  to  assist  with  Municipal  Separate  Storm  Sewer  System (MS4) Discharge Permit requirements.

Storm Drain Trash Screens Phase Two

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  was  identified  in  the  City’s  EWMP.

1213fedd-db49-47c8-aa04-70050fc8c3df.label: text_chunk
1213fedd-db49-47c8-aa04-70050fc8c3df.text:
This project includes the installation of trash screens at the City’s storm drain inlets to prevent debris from entering the storm drain system. The proposed trash screens  comply  with  the  Trash  Total  Maximum  Daily  Load  (TMDLs) requirements.

City Traffic Signals Backup Power

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  will  include  upgrading  the  backup  power system to the City’s traffic signals on Civic Center Way.

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.

Outdoor Warning Sirens - Design (FEMA Project)

Outdoor Warning Sirens - Design (FEMA Project) ¾ Project Description:

  An Emergency Warning Siren system will improve the City’s  ability  to  alert  the

b5672c4f-7042-4ad5-8405-8ee34540af5c.label: text_chunk
b5672c4f-7042-4ad5-8405-8ee34540af5c.text: community  by  providing  mechanisms  to  alert residents in areas without power and those who live in neighborhoods with poor  cell  service.    Phase  One  of  the  project  included  a  sound  study  to determine the optimal number and locations of individual sirens, power and infrastructure  requirements,  and  siren  sound  range.

58b662ad-e266-4ad0-83f7-a1907589b7db.label: text_chunk
58b662ad-e266-4ad0-83f7-a1907589b7db.text: The  City  has  been awarded a FEMA Hazard Mitigation grant to fund the design, engineering and environmental compliance needed for a shovel ready project.  Phase Two of the Emergency Warning Sirens project will be implementation of the design plan,  including  purchasing,  installing,  and  testing  the  sirens.    The  City  will apply for future grant funding for this phase of the project.

5030bbaf-432f-476c-a1bc-4c64531e970f.label: text_chunk
5030bbaf-432f-476c-a1bc-4c64531e970f.text:

  City  Council  approved  an  agreement  with Acoustic Technology to design the outdoor warning sirens.  A design report will be presented to Council upon completion.  Comments  were  sent  back  to  the  Consultant  and  staff  met  with  the consultant to discuss those comments earlier this month.

community_summary.0:
**Summary of Relationships:**

Troy Spayd is actively involved with the Public Works Commission, having prepared the agenda report for their meeting. This highlights his significant role in the commission's activities, showcasing his contributions to the planning and organization of their discussions. The relationship emphasizes his responsibility and engagement within the commission's operations.
community_summary.1: In the context of the Public Works Commission, Rob DuBoux, serving as the Public Works Director, played a pivotal role by approving a report that was prepared for the commission. This action underscores his authority and active involvement in the decision-making processes of the commission. The relationship highlights DuBoux's significant influence and responsibility within the framework of public works governance.
community_summary.2:
The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **Marie Canyon Green Streets**: This project is in the **Construction** phase, which is essential for implementing its designs through physical construction activities.

2. **Emergency Warning Sirens Project**: This initiative is being executed by the **City**, highlighting the City's responsibility for this important safety measure.

3. **Broad Beach Road**: This location is directly associated with the **CalJPIA Project**, which focuses on repairing biofiltration facilities along the road, indicating a significant connection between the project and the area.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the critical roles of construction and municipal responsibility in urban development and safety initiatives, as well as the specific geographical focus of environmental repair efforts.

community_summary.5: The PCH Signal Synchronization System Improvements Project is set to be presented to the Planning Commission for approval. This indicates a direct relationship between the project and the Planning Commission, which is the governmental body responsible for overseeing such initiatives. community_summary.6: The relationship between Caltrans and the Project is centered around the approval process. This approval indicates that Caltrans plays a significant part in overseeing and facilitating the project's progression.
community_summary.7: The relationship between Measure M and the Westward Beach Road Repair Project highlights a significant funding eligibility issue. Initially, Measure M was designated to provide financial support for the repair project; however, it ultimately became ineligible due to modifications enacted by the City Council. This change underscores the impact of local governance on funding allocations for community infrastructure projects.
community_summary.8: The Civic Center Water Treatment Facility Phase 2 is financially linked to the Fiscal Year 2022-2023 through the inclusion of its revised project budget in that fiscal year's budget. This relationship underscores the significance of the project's funding and its alignment with the financial planning of the specified fiscal year.
community_summary.9: The State Water Board is actively involved in providing grants, which are supported by the budget. 1. **Permanent Skate Park** is designed to be constructed on **Crummer/Case Court**, indicating that this parcel is the designated site for the skate park.
2. The **Permanent Skate Park** is also located **adjacent to Malibu Bluffs Park**, suggesting a close proximity that may enhance the recreational opportunities available in the area.

2. **Vehicle Protection Devices** are required to be installed in the **City's right-of-way** to safeguard outdoor pedestrian seating areas from potential vehicle impacts.

Overall, the summary emphasizes the importance of vehicle protection devices as a safety measure mandated by local regulations, specifically focusing on their role in protecting pedestrians and outdoor seating areas within the city's jurisdiction.

The compliance with these standards is crucial for the project's approval and implementation, highlighting the importance of regulatory adherence in public infrastructure development.

community_summary.23: The relationship between Raised Medians and PCH indicates that raised medians are set to be installed on Pacific Coast Highway (PCH), particularly in locations marked by double yellow lines. This installation aims to enhance road safety and traffic management in these specific areas.
community_summary.24: The relationships highlight the significance of the PCH Crosswalk Improvements project, which is directly informed by the findings of the 2015 PCH Safety Study. Specifically, the improvements are focused on the area surrounding 20326 PCH, where new safety features are set to be installed. The synthesis of these relationships underscores the project's foundation in research and its targeted approach to enhancing safety in a critical location.

This project underscores the significance of integrating ecological solutions into urban planning.
community_summary.26: The relationship between the Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (MS4) and the City’s Enhanced Watershed Management Program (EWMP) centers on compliance with discharge permit requirements. community_summary.27: The relationship between Trash Screens and the Storm Drain System is crucial for maintaining the functionality and integrity of urban drainage systems. Trash Screens are installed at the inlets of the Storm Drain System specifically to prevent debris from entering. This protective measure ensures that the storm drain system operates efficiently, safeguarding it from potential blockages and damage caused by debris accumulation.
community_summary.28: The relationship between Traffic Signals and the Backup Power System centers on a project aimed at upgrading the backup power system. The emphasis on ensuring operational continuity during outages highlights the significance of this relationship in promoting safer and more efficient traffic flow.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight key interactions and funding allocations among various entities involved in disaster recovery and cultural resource management.

1. **Budget and Cultural Resources Soil Investigation**: The budget is allocated to fund the Cultural Resources Soil Investigation project, indicating a financial commitment to assess and preserve cultural resources in the area.

2. **FEMA and Storm Drain Master Plan**: FEMA is providing funding for the Storm Drain Master Plan project as part of its disaster recovery efforts following the Woolsey Fire. This emphasizes FEMA's role in supporting infrastructure improvements to enhance safety and resilience in the aftermath of natural disasters.

3. **Collaboration between FEMA and CalOES**: FEMA coordinates with CalOES (California Office of Emergency Services) to manage emergency responses and safety projects in California. This collaboration underscores the importance of joint efforts in disaster management and recovery initiatives.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of funding, project management, and collaborative efforts in addressing cultural resource preservation and disaster recovery in California.

community_summary.33: The Woolsey Fire has a significant relationship with the Storm Drain Master Plan, as it caused the development of this plan. This relationship highlights the impact of environmental disasters on urban planning and infrastructure development.

The relationships highlight the impact of the Woolsey Fire on local infrastructure and the subsequent repair projects initiated in response. 

1. **Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements**: This project is aimed at repairing the damage inflicted by the Woolsey Fire, indicating a direct response to the fire's destructive effects on the drainage system.

2. **Point Dume Decomposed Granite Walkway Repair Project**: Similarly, this project was initiated due to the damage caused by the Woolsey Fire to the sidewalks, emphasizing the fire's widespread impact on community pathways.

In summary, both repair projects—the Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements and the Point Dume Decomposed Granite Walkway Repair—are direct responses to the damage caused by the Woolsey Fire, underscoring the fire's significant impact on local infrastructure.
